content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of length NNN.

The array can be modified by performing the following operation on it:


Choose an index iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N).
Then, for each index jjj (1‚Č§j‚Č§N1 \le j \le N1‚Č§j‚Č§N),

If j<ij \lt ij<i, add AiA_iAi‚Äč to AjA_jAj‚Äč.
If j‚Č•ij \ge ij‚Č•i, subtract AiA_iAi‚Äč from AjA_jAj‚Äč instead.


Note that every element of AAA must be modified - you cannot leave elements unchanged.

For example, if A=[2,4,‚ąí1,3]A = [2, 4, -1, 3]A=[2,4,‚ąí1,3] and we choose i=2i = 2i=2, the array becomes [6,0,‚ąí5,‚ąí1][6, 0, -5, -1][6,0,‚ąí5,‚ąí1] because A2=4A_2 = 4A2‚Äč=4 so we add 444 to A1A_1A1‚Äč and subtract 444 from A2,A3,A4A_2, A_3, A_4A2‚Äč,A3‚Äč,A4‚Äč.

You would like to make all the elements of AAA equal by performing the given operation several times (possibly, zero times).

Is it possible to achieve this goal?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚ÄĒ the length of the array AAA.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer: YES\texttt{YES}YES if it's possible to make all the elements of AAA equal after performing several operations, and NO\texttt{NO}NO otherwise.


Each character of the output may be printed in any case: for example, the strings NO,no,No\texttt{NO}, \texttt{no}, \texttt{No}NO,no,No and nO\texttt{nO}nO will all be treated as equivalent.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: We can choose i=2i = 2i=2, and the operation affects the array as follows:

A2=‚ąí1A_2 = -1A2‚Äč=‚ąí1.
So, we add ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 to A1A_1A1‚Äč and subtract ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 from A2A_2A2‚Äč.
The array becomes [0,0][0, 0][0,0], all its elements are now equal.

Test case 222: It can be shown that no sequence of operations can make all the elements become equal.
Test case 333: All the elements are already equal.
